title: Authoring Documents
description: Write company SOPs and policies from templates, cite regulations as you draft, and move them through review with an audit trail.

steps.caption: Your company documents
steps.narration: The Author tab is where your team writes company documents — SOPs, policies, permits and standards — organised into project folders.

steps.caption: Start from a template
steps.narration: Start a new document from a template — like an ISO 9001 standard operating procedure — so it's structured correctly from the very first draft.

steps.caption: Write in the editor
steps.narration: Write and format in the built-in editor — headings, tables and lists, just like a word processor, but versioned and fully audited.

steps.caption: Crosswalk clauses to regulations
steps.narration: Open the Clause Crosswalk to map each section of your document to the exact regulation clause it satisfies — the traceability matrix auditors expect.

steps.caption: Link to regulations and assets
steps.narration: Link the document to the regulations and assets it covers, so it's connected to your compliance picture.

steps.caption: Review and sign off
steps.narration: Add review comments, then move it through the approval workflow with an electronic signature and a full audit trail.
